facts.text: Vilano Beach’s Magic Beach Hotel, once the 1951 Vilano Beach Motel, doubled as a set for the 1999 TV series Safe Harbor starring Rue McClanahan.
An eight‑foot‑tall orange bird once flew over Vilano Beach to advertise Florida’s citrus industry before highways cut through the area.
A neon sign of bunnies leaping from a hat pays tribute to the motel owner’s husband, a magician who once performed in town.
Newt and Wilda Haley rescued the citrus bird, painted it blue, and added a “Newt’s Motel” logo to help travelers spot the landmark.
Just north of Historic Downtown St. Augustine, Vilano Beach blends Old Florida charm with quirky, small‑town vibes.
Vilano Beach is a popular spot for swimming, surfing, and paddleboarding, but always check current conditions and lifeguard stations.
